nodes.context: Bootlegging and racketeering gang led by Dutch Schultz and Joey Noe, based in New York City in the 1920s-1930s.
Governing body of organized crime in the US, also known as the Commission.
The five major Italian crime families in New York City.
Irish-American organized crime group led by Jack 'Legs' Diamond.
Jewish gangster enforcement arm of the National Crime Syndicate.
Confederation of mobsters across the US.
Governing body of the American Mafia that ordered Dutch Schultz's murder.
Criminal organization led by Dutch Schultz, involved in bootlegging and numbers racket.

edges.evidence_quote: Schultz asked the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia, for permission to kill Dewey
edges.context: Schultz sought permission from the Commission, indicating a subordinate relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: this brazen move led to a bootleg war with New York's Irish mob, led by Jack 'Legs' Diamond
edges.context: The Noe-Schultz gang fought a bootleg war against the Irish mob.

edges.evidence_quote: the Commission ordered Louis Buchalter to eliminate 'The Dutchman'
edges.context: Murder, Inc. carried out the Commission's order to kill Dutch Schultz.

edges.evidence_quote: Schultz had proposed to the National Crime Syndicate, a confederation of mobsters, that Dewey be murdered
edges.context: Schultz proposed a murder to the National Crime Syndicate, indicating membership or association.

edges.evidence_quote: Allegiance: New York City's Five Families
edges.context: The infobox lists the Five Families as the allegiance of the Noe-Schultz Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Schultz asked the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia, for permission to kill Dewey, in an attempt to avert his conviction. They refused. When Schultz disobeyed them and made an attempt to kill Dewey, the Commission ordered his murder in 1935.
edges.context: The Commission ordered the murder of Dutch Schultz after he disobeyed their refusal to kill Dewey.
